taxonomy: Siloxerus pygmaeus belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a genus of small plants commonly known as dwarf parsley. The classification of Siloxerus pygmaeus places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The plant is native to Australia, specifically in Western Australia, where it grows in sandy soils. Further classification of Siloxerus pygmaeus places it in the order Apiales.
